steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage presents North American and international studies (including Finland) showing a positive association between dissolved organic carbon and mercury. The Brazil study is the outlier — few studies matched its result. The question asks what conclusion is best supported by that distribution.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — positive association particular to North America: contradicted by Finland's findings confirming the same pattern. ✗
  • Choice B — Pyhtilä's study had measurement problems: Pyhtilä's study actually confirmed the positive association — no measurement issues mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — positive association is typical: when most studies show a pattern and few show the opposite, the pattern is typical. ✓
  • Choice D — absolute concentration levels: the passage focuses on correlation, not absolute levels. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
